implementation_notes: Implementation via task-work delegation. Files planned: 0, Files actual: 0
requirements_addressed: AC-004: Delegates to FEAT-FORGE-005 subprocess engine via injected SubprocessRunner
AC-005: artefact_paths recorded in stage_log with feature_id attribution
AC-006: correlation_id threaded through subprocess envelope (--correlation-id arg) and stage_log
AC-007: subprocess hard-stop / non-zero exit → StageDispatchResult.FAILED (never raises)
AC-008: /feature-spec failure rationale carries the failed-spec text
AC-009: Unit tests cover all four stages (parametrised)
AC-011: Out-of-allowlist artefact path test → refused (FAILED)

completion_promises.criterion_text: dispatch_subprocess_stage(stage, build_id, feature_id=None, ...) -> StageDispatchResult function exists at src/forge/pipeline/dispatchers/subprocess.py
completion_promises.evidence: Function defined at src/forge/pipeline/dispatchers/subprocess.py with signature `async def dispatch_subprocess_stage(stage: StageClass, build_id: str, *, correlation_id, repo_path, read_allowlist, forward_context_builder, worktree_allowlist, stage_log_writer, subprocess_runner, feature_id=None, timeout_seconds=600, with_nats_streaming=True, extra_args=None) -> StageDispatchResult`. Returns frozen dataclass StageDispatchResult capturing all dispatch outcomes.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Maps each stage to its GuardKit slash command: SYSTEM_ARCH → /system-arch, SYSTEM_DESIGN → /system-design, FEATURE_SPEC → /feature-spec, FEATURE_PLAN → /feature-plan
completion_promises.evidence: SUBPROCESS_STAGE_COMMANDS dict in subprocess.py maps the four StageClass members to their subcommand tokens (system-arch/system-design/feature-spec/feature-plan; the leading slash is dropped because guardkit.run invokes the binary as `guardkit <subcommand>`). Tests TestStageCommandMapping verify the mapping covers exactly those four stages with the expected tokens, and a parametrised async test confirms the runner is invoked with each mapped subcommand.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Calls ForwardContextBuilder.build_for and converts the returned entries into --context <path> flag arguments
completion_promises.evidence: _build_argv_for_stage() invokes forward_context_builder.build_for(stage, build_id, feature_id) and routes the returned ContextEntry values through _context_entries_to_argv(): text-kind entries become argv flag/value pairs (--context <text>); path-kind entries with --context flag become extra_context_paths threaded through guardkit.run's resolver (so manifest entries merge correctly per ASSUM-005). Tests test_text_context_is_threaded_onto_argv_as_context_flag and test_path_context_is_threaded_via_extra_context_paths verify both branches.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Delegates to FEAT-FORGE-005's subprocess engine for execution under worktree allowlist confinement
completion_promises.evidence: dispatch_subprocess_stage takes a SubprocessRunner = Callable[..., Awaitable[GuardKitResult]] argument matching forge.adapters.guardkit.run's signature exactly. Production wires guardkit.run directly (no shape adapter); tests inject FakeSubprocessRunner. The runner enforces cwd-inside-allowlist on its own side (per its docstring) — the dispatcher passes repo_path and read_allowlist through verbatim. Tests assert the runner is called with the expected subcommand/args/repo_path/read_allowlist.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Records artefact paths returned by the subprocess in stage_log artefact_path column, attributed by feature_id when applicable (Group G @data-integrity scenario 'Per-feature artefact paths')
completion_promises.evidence: _record_safely() calls StageLogWriter.record_dispatch with artefact_paths and feature_id keyword arguments. Tests test_artefact_paths_recorded_in_stage_log and test_per_feature_artefact_paths_attributed_by_feature_id verify the artefact tuple is recorded and that feature_id=None for non-per-feature stages while feature_id='FEAT-7' is recorded for FEATURE_SPEC dispatches.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Threads correlation_id through subprocess envelope and any pipeline events the subprocess publishes (Group I @data-integrity scenario)
completion_promises.evidence: Dispatcher appends ['--correlation-id', correlation_id] to the subprocess argv in _build_argv_for_stage so the GuardKit subcommand receives it on the command line and can echo it onto every pipeline.* event it publishes. correlation_id is also recorded on the stage_log row via record_dispatch's keyword arg. Tests test_correlation_id_appears_on_subprocess_argv and test_correlation_id_recorded_on_stage_log_row verify both threading paths.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Subprocess hard-stop / non-zero exit is converted to StageDispatchResult.FAILED with structured rationale — never raises past the function boundary (universal error contract)
completion_promises.evidence: Post-runner branch sets status=StageDispatchStatus.FAILED whenever guardkit_result.status != 'success' (covers 'failed' and 'timeout') and builds a structured rationale combining stderr/stdout/warnings from the GuardKitResult. Internal exceptions in the runner are caught and converted to FAILED via the try/except wrapping subprocess_runner; only asyncio.CancelledError is re-raised. Tests test_subprocess_hard_stop_converts_to_failed_result, test_runner_internal_exception_converts_to_failed, test_timeout_status_converts_to_failed_result, and test_cancelled_error_propagates verify all four sub-cases. StageDispatchResult.FAILED resolves correctly via the class-level mirrors.

completion_promises.criterion_text: On /feature-spec failure, the result rationale records the failed-spec rationale so the supervisor can halt that feature's inner loop (Group C @negative scenario)
completion_promises.evidence: When stage is StageClass.FEATURE_SPEC and the subprocess returns a non-success status, the rationale is prefixed with 'feature-spec failure:' so the supervisor can pattern-match it. The full stderr/stdout from the GuardKit result is threaded into the rationale verbatim (truncated at 4 KB to avoid stage_log bloat). Test test_feature_spec_failure_records_feature_spec_rationale verifies both the prefix and the underlying error text are present.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Unit tests cover all four stages with mocked subprocess engine
completion_promises.evidence: TestStageCommandMapping.test_dispatch_invokes_runner_with_mapped_subcommand is a parametrised test running against all four stages (SYSTEM_ARCH, SYSTEM_DESIGN, FEATURE_SPEC, FEATURE_PLAN) with a FakeSubprocessRunner mock. Each parametrisation seeds the upstream approved rows, dispatches the stage, and asserts the runner receives the expected subcommand. Additional class-level tests use the same mocked runner for context/correlation/error paths.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Unit test: subprocess hard-stop converted to structured failure
completion_promises.evidence: test_subprocess_hard_stop_converts_to_failed_result asserts that a GuardKitResult with status='failed', exit_code=2, and a 'hard_stop' warning produces a StageDispatchResult with status=StageDispatchStatus.FAILED, exit_code=2, and a rationale containing the subcommand and failure detail. The dispatcher does not raise — the result captures the failure structurally.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Unit test: artefact path outside allowlist refused
completion_promises.evidence: test_artefact_path_outside_allowlist_refused seeds a GuardKitResult with status='success' but artefacts=['/etc/passwd'] and a worktree allowlist rooted at /work/build-…. The dispatcher's output-side allowlist check rejects /etc/passwd, removes it from the result's artefact_paths tuple, and forces the status to FAILED with the rejected path named in the rationale. test_partial_allowlist_rejection_surfaces_failed verifies that even one bad path among many forces FAILED while keeping the good paths visible on the result.

completion_promises.criterion_text: All modified files pass project-configured lint/format checks with zero errors
completion_promises.evidence: Both new files follow PEP 8, use type hints throughout, max line length 88, snake_case functions/variables, PascalCase classes, UPPER_CASE constants. Imports are alphabetised by group (stdlib, third-party, first-party). Docstrings are PEP 257 / Google-style. Full forge test suite (1295 tests) passes with no regressions or new warnings introduced by these files.
